title: notes/
body:
# notes/

Synthesis notes that connect papers around a focused topic.

https://lit-template.sepo-preview.xyz/notes/

<!-- sha1: f649ef1705e216ffb06e9d9f5c2a46e151d222b8 -->

nodes.body:
I have enough context to answer this as usage guidance for the Notes page rather than making code changes. The key pattern is: durable Markdown lives under `content/`, and `/notes/` is a folder index for synthesis notes.`/notes/` is for **synthesis notes**: topic-level notes that connect multiple papers, methods, or research questions.

To use it:

1. Add a new Markdown file under [`content/notes/`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/tree/main/content/notes), for example `content/notes/my-topic.md`.

2. Use this frontmatter pattern, like [`content/notes/example-topic.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/main/content/notes/example-topic.md):

```md
---
title: "My topic"
type: note
tags:
  - note
  - synthesis
---

Write the synthesis here.

## Linked papers

- [Paper title](../papers/example-citekey.md) [@examplecitekey]
```

3. Link to paper notes from [`content/papers/`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/tree/main/content/papers). Paper citations use BibTeX citekeys from [`bibliography.bib`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/main/bibliography.bib).

4. Add the new note slug to [`content/notes/_meta.json`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/main/content/notes/_meta.json) so it appears in the `/notes/` listing in the order you want:

```json
{
  "label": "Notes",
  "pages": ["example-topic", "attention-patterns", "my-topic"]
}
```

5. Preview locally with:

```bash
npm ci
npm run install-plugins
npm run dev
```

If you want Sepo to add a paper for you, use the **Agent / Add Paper** GitHub Actions workflow with a paper URL, DOI, arXiv ID, title, or BibTeX entry. It will open a PR updating the paper note and bibliography.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `high`_
